exercises.prompt: Show the top 10 Pokémon by total stats — name and total, highest first. Break ties alphabetically by name.
exercises.referenceSql: SELECT name, total FROM pokemon ORDER BY total DESC, name LIMIT 10
hints: You need a sort (highest first) and a way to keep only the first ten rows.
To break ties alphabetically, add a second sort column: ORDER BY total DESC, name.

```sql
SELECT name, total FROM pokemon ORDER BY total DESC, name LIMIT 10
```

exercises.prompt: Find the 5 heaviest Pokémon — name and weight_kg, heaviest first (ties alphabetical by name).
exercises.referenceSql: SELECT name, weight_kg FROM pokemon ORDER BY weight_kg DESC, name LIMIT 5
hints: Same shape as the last exercise, but sorting on weight_kg.
ORDER BY weight_kg DESC, name — then LIMIT 5.

```sql
SELECT name, weight_kg FROM pokemon ORDER BY weight_kg DESC, name LIMIT 5
```

exercises.prompt: Show the 5 Pokémon with the lowest total stats — name and total, lowest first. Break ties alphabetically by name.
exercises.referenceSql: SELECT name, total FROM pokemon ORDER BY total ASC, name LIMIT 5
hints: This time the smallest totals come first — sort ascending, then keep the first five rows.
Ascending is the default direction, but be explicit and add the tiebreaker: ORDER BY total ASC, name — then LIMIT 5.

```sql
SELECT name, total FROM pokemon ORDER BY total ASC, name LIMIT 5
```

exercises.prompt: List the name, attack, and defense of every dragon-type (type1) Pokémon, sorted by attack descending, then by defense descending, then by name ascending.
exercises.referenceSql: SELECT name, attack, defense FROM pokemon WHERE type1 = 'dragon' ORDER BY attack DESC, defense DESC, name ASC
hints: When two Pokémon tie on the first sort column, add more sort columns to decide the order.
Filter to type1 = 'dragon', then ORDER BY attack DESC, defense DESC, name ASC.

```sql
SELECT name, attack, defense FROM pokemon WHERE type1 = 'dragon' ORDER BY attack DESC, defense DESC, name ASC
```

exercises.prompt: Among legendary Pokémon, show the 5 with the highest attack — name and attack, highest first. Break ties alphabetically by name.
exercises.referenceSql: SELECT name, attack FROM pokemon WHERE is_legendary ORDER BY attack DESC, name LIMIT 5
hints: First keep only legendary Pokémon, then sort by attack and take the top five.
WHERE is_legendary, then ORDER BY attack DESC, name LIMIT 5.

```sql
SELECT name, attack FROM pokemon WHERE is_legendary ORDER BY attack DESC, name LIMIT 5
```

exercises.prompt: Among Pokémon with a total stat score of at least 600, show the 10 highest — name, total, and speed — sorted by total descending, then speed descending, then name ascending.
exercises.referenceSql: SELECT name, total, speed FROM pokemon WHERE total >= 600 ORDER BY total DESC, speed DESC, name ASC LIMIT 10
hints: Filter to the high-total Pokémon first, then apply a three-level sort and cap the rows.
WHERE total >= 600, then ORDER BY total DESC, speed DESC, name ASC LIMIT 10.

```sql
SELECT name, total, speed FROM pokemon WHERE total >= 600 ORDER BY total DESC, speed DESC, name ASC LIMIT 10
```
